warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Method provenance block
Property: Fukui electrophilic descriptor (f⁺) at the most electrophilic site of furan (atom 5, C)
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) in PySCF 2.x
Geometry provenance: user-supplied furan.xyz; single-point Fukui evaluation (no further geometry relaxation)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1). The N±1 states were treated as doublets (±1 charge, multiplicity 2) as required for the Fukui formalism.
Software backend: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill calling PySCF for DFT single-points; Mulliken charges obtained via PySCF.
Convergence & caveats: All three SCFs (neutral, cation, anion) reported convergence. Integrity checks flagged the usual Fukui charge-conservation warnings (sums of f⁺ and f⁻ = 1.0000) but within tolerance. No other warnings.
Files & reproducibility: The live engine log was streamed to a `fukui_*.out` file in the working directory during execution (available for inspection), and the full JSON result was saved alongside the plot (`agent_call_01_plot.png`).

Headline result
Most electrophilic site: atom 5 (C), f⁺ = 1.9242931 e.
Most nucleophilic site: atom 5 (same C), f⁻ = 0.1616371 e.
Dual descriptor and full per-atom f values are in the JSON for further analysis.

The above values are computed, not experimental.
